rationale.AWR: University Health Services runs structured triage, embedded counselors and the SilverCloud/UWELL wellbeing program; some proactive detection but staff-side and contingent-worker monitoring is thinner. Signal: formal counseling pathways + UWELL.
rationale.EMP: Standard student ombuds and victim-advocacy services exist; harm-acknowledgment is process-led rather than affective. Signal: ombuds office + dean of students advocacy.
rationale.ACT: UHS counseling meets demand for routine cases but, like most large publics, has reported wait-time pressure; emergency aid (Dean of Students Crisis Loans/Open Seat food pantry) exists. Signal: crisis grants + counseling capacity.
rationale.EQU: Bucky's Tuition Promise covers in-state Pell-eligible students and FastForward retention support; completion gaps narrowing but persistent. Signal: Bucky's Tuition Promise.
rationale.BND: TAA (oldest US grad-worker union) gives contractual protections, but Wisconsin Act 10 stripped most public-sector bargaining, weakening enforceable boundaries for staff. Signal: Act 10 erosion of bargaining.
rationale.ACC: Title IX office and Clery reporting are functional; no recent landmark OCR resolution against the institution found, response is compliant rather than self-disclosing. Signal: compliant Title IX/Clery posture.
rationale.SYS: Wisconsin Idea framing and public-good advocacy are genuine, but state-level funding fights constrain structural investment. Signal: Wisconsin Idea public mission.
rationale.INT: Strong public mission rhetoric; AAUP censure history and Act-10 era governance erosion create some values-behavior gap. Signal: shared-governance erosion.

name: University of Science and Technology of China

rationale.AWR: Psychological counseling centers are now mandated at Chinese universities and USTC operates one, but disaggregated detection data is not publicly disclosed; scored conservatively under low English-language evidence. Signal: MOE-mandated counseling center.
rationale.EMP: No public ombuds-style independent advocacy disclosure; supervisor-power asymmetry is a documented systemic risk across Chinese grad programs. Signal: hierarchical supervisor model.
rationale.ACT: Counseling and emergency-aid provisions exist per national policy but utilization/wait-time data is not public. Conservative score, low confidence.
rationale.EQU: National financial-aid and rural-quota schemes give USTC students baseline access support; institution-specific equity reporting is limited. Signal: national-grant access scheme.
rationale.BND: Graduate labor conditions in China carry documented abusive-supervision and overwork risk; no enforceable union mechanism. Signal: documented abusive-supervision risk (BMC Psychology 2023).
rationale.ACC: Limited public mechanism for harm acknowledgment/repair; whistleblower protection is weak in the sector. Signal: weak public accountability disclosure.
rationale.SYS: Strong long-horizon state research planning, but independent structural critique of the system that produces student suffering is constrained. Signal: state planning without independent critique.
rationale.INT: Values alignment cannot be independently verified; scored conservatively. Low confidence due to limited English-language/public disclosure.

rationale.AWR: Habif Health & Wellness Center and Uncle Joe's peer counseling provide layered detection; staff-side monitoring less visible. Signal: Habif + peer counseling.
rationale.EMP: Title IX and victim-support offices operate professionally; affective resonance reported as mixed by grad workers. Signal: structured support offices.
rationale.ACT: Counseling expanded with TimelyCare-style virtual access; emergency aid funds and a $15 campus minimum won partly via WUGWU pressure. Signal: expanded counseling access.
rationale.EQU: WashU moved to need-blind/no-loan aid and grew Pell share after long criticism for low low-income enrollment; adjuncts unionized (SEIU 2015). Signal: need-blind shift + SEIU adjunct contract.
rationale.BND: WUGWU graduate workers remain unrecognized by the administration, limiting enforceable labor protections; adjunct CBA exists. Signal: refusal to recognize WUGWU.
rationale.ACC: Functional Title IX/Clery compliance; no landmark adverse OCR resolution found. Signal: compliant posture.
rationale.SYS: Pell-access reform shows root-cause orientation on affordability; structural advocacy is moderate. Signal: affordability reform.
rationale.INT: Large endowment and historically low low-income access created a values gap now partly addressed; non-recognition of grad union is an ongoing integrity tension. Signal: endowment-vs-access tension.

name: Hong Kong University of Science and Technology

rationale.AWR: Counseling & Wellness Center and a published mental-health framework ('A State of Well Being') indicate proactive, English-medium detection. Signal: institutional mental-health strategy publication.
rationale.EMP: Student support is structured and English-accessible; advocacy independence less documented. Signal: counseling and wellness center.
rationale.ACT: Well-resourced counseling and wellbeing programming for a small student body. Signal: low student-to-counselor ratio relative to peers.
rationale.EQU: Need-based aid and international diversity are strong; local widening-access metrics less public. Signal: financial-aid provision.
rationale.BND: Postgraduate stipends are competitive but RPg workload protections and consent disclosures are thinner; English-medium aids transparency. Signal: RPg stipend support.
rationale.ACC: Compliance-oriented; operates under Hong Kong's national-security regime which constrains independent harm disclosure on contested issues. Signal: NSL operating context (HRW 2024).
rationale.SYS: Strong research planning but structural critique/advocacy is constrained by the post-2020 academic-freedom environment. Signal: academic-freedom decline (HRW).
rationale.INT: Internal welfare practices appear genuine; the binding constraint on values-expression is the external NSL context, not institutional choice — scored conservatively, medium confidence.

rationale.AWR: Swedish work-environment law mandates psychosocial monitoring and KI runs occupational-health surveillance; medical-school setting raises trainee-distress awareness. Signal: AML-mandated work-environment surveys.
rationale.EMP: Student/doctoral support exists but the Macchiarini era exposed weak institutional empathy toward those who raised concerns. Signal: documented whistleblower mistreatment.
rationale.ACT: Counseling and doctoral-support provisions are solid in the Swedish welfare context. Signal: student health service.
rationale.EQU: Swedish tuition-free model and strong PhD-as-employee status give robust baseline equity (paid, unionized doctoral positions). Signal: doctoral candidates are salaried employees.
rationale.BND: Doctoral employee status gives strong labor boundaries; consent/research-ethics boundaries were severely breached in the Macchiarini case. Signal: salaried PhD model vs. ethics breach.
rationale.ACC: The Macchiarini scandal showed denial, delayed acknowledgment, and retaliation against whistleblowers before external compulsion forced findings — a serious accountability failure, partly corrected by post-2016 reforms and the VC resignation. Signal: Macchiarini whistleblower retaliation.
rationale.SYS: Post-scandal it built new research-integrity and ethics structures, showing systemic learning over time. Signal: post-2016 integrity reforms.
rationale.INT: Stated values were contradicted under reputational pressure during the scandal; reforms since show partial recovery but the integrity breach is adjudicated and severe. Signal: values-collapse under pressure.

rationale.AWR: Student wellbeing and counseling services exist, but the 2022-23 Inspectorate process showed the institution under-detected a culture of social unsafety. Signal: Inspectorate social-safety probe.
rationale.EMP: The Inspectorate found employees afraid to speak and victims drawing 'the short straw,' indicating weak validation of harm reports. Signal: 148 unsafety reports, victims unsupported.
rationale.ACT: Has since published a social-safety action plan with concrete measures after ministerial pressure. Signal: post-2024 social-safety action plan.
rationale.EQU: Dutch system gives strong baseline access; PhD candidates are salaried employees. Signal: salaried PhD model + Dutch access.
rationale.BND: PhD candidates report disproportionate exposure to social-unsafety incidents from senior staff; employee status gives formal but unevenly enforced protection. Signal: PhDs hit 'disproportionately often.'
rationale.ACC: Education Inspectorate found TU Delft 'neglects' and 'mismanages' social safety and the minister threatened to intervene — a serious external accountability finding; the university accepted findings and committed to reform. Signal: Inspectorate adverse finding 2024.
rationale.SYS: Reform plan shows structural intent on culture change; durability untested. Signal: structural social-safety program.
rationale.INT: Documented gap between stated inclusive values and a 'culture of fear' identified by an independent regulator. Signal: regulator-identified values gap.

rationale.AWR: Standard counseling and wellbeing services exist, but an internal report flagged psychological risks from the restructure that management was slow to act on. Signal: internal psychosocial-risk report.
rationale.EMP: Staff describe the 'Renew ANU' process as causing 'irreparable mental harm'; affective care toward staff was widely reported as poor during 2024-25. Signal: staff testimony of harm.
rationale.ACT: Wellbeing supports offered, but action lagged the documented distress; NTEU and Comcare WHS complaints filed. Signal: Comcare WHS complaints.
rationale.EQU: Strong Indigenous-access programs (Tjabal Centre) and Australian equity frameworks; commonwealth-supported access. Signal: Indigenous access programs.
rationale.BND: Renew ANU targeted ~$100M in staff cuts (NTEU est. 638 FTE threatened), creating acute depletion and insecurity for workers. Signal: mass restructure / job cuts.
rationale.ACC: Staff raised work-health-and-safety concerns with the regulator Comcare; leadership made some concessions (no further compulsory cuts) under pressure rather than self-initiated. Signal: regulator-escalated WHS concerns.
rationale.SYS: Public-good and policy-research mission is genuine; but the restructure prioritized deficit reduction over structural staff wellbeing. Signal: deficit-driven planning.
rationale.INT: Sharp gap between stated care-for-staff values and a restructure described by staff as causing terror/harm; integrity scored low. Signal: values-vs-restructure gap.

name: London School of Economics and Political Science

rationale.AWR: LSE LIFE and student wellbeing services provide structured support; staff-side casualisation monitoring weaker. Signal: student wellbeing service + LSE LIFE.
rationale.EMP: Student support is professionalized; the historic outsourced-cleaners dispute showed empathy gaps toward low-power workers later partly resolved. Signal: cleaners' dispute resolution.
rationale.ACT: Counseling and disability/wellbeing provision is reasonably resourced for a small institution. Signal: wellbeing service capacity.
rationale.EQU: Strong widening-participation and bursary programs (UK access agreements via OfS); decolonising-curriculum work. Signal: OfS access-and-participation plan.
rationale.BND: High casualisation (one-in-three UK academic workers temporary) and prior outsourcing of migrant cleaners are real boundary concerns; cleaners eventually brought in-house after sustained struggle. Signal: casualisation + outsourcing legacy.
rationale.ACC: After the UVW cleaners' strike, LSE conceded in-housing and improved terms — a documented correction under pressure. Signal: in-housing concession.
rationale.SYS: As a social-science institution it does substantial structural-policy research and public advocacy on inequality. Signal: structural research mission.
rationale.INT: Eventually aligned with stated equality values on cleaners, but only after prolonged campaigning — correction was reactive, not self-initiated. Signal: reactive values alignment.

rationale.AWR: Member grandes écoles (incl. École Polytechnique) run student health/psychological services; the military-affiliated Polytechnique culture has faced bizutage/hazing and harassment scrutiny. Signal: école health services + hazing scrutiny.
rationale.EMP: Reporting cells for sexual violence (CEVSS-type) exist across French écoles, but cultural empathy in elite-grande-école settings is contested. Signal: VSS reporting cells.
rationale.ACT: Counseling and social-services provision per French norms; institution-specific wait-time data not public. Conservative, low confidence.
rationale.EQU: French écoles face long-standing social-reproduction critique (low scholarship-student share); some widening programs exist. Signal: grande-école social-selectivity critique.
rationale.BND: French doctoral contracts give salaried status and statutory protections; consent/disclosure frameworks standardized nationally. Signal: salaried doctoral contracts.
rationale.ACC: École Polytechnique has faced public scrutiny over harassment handling; accountability disclosure is limited in English. Signal: limited public harm disclosure.
rationale.SYS: Strong state research/innovation planning; structural critique of own selectivity is limited. Signal: state-aligned planning.
rationale.INT: Values alignment hard to verify in English-language sources; scored conservatively with low confidence given the recent (2019) federation and limited English disclosure.

rationale.AWR: Student psychologists, study advisers and confidential advisers form a layered detection system, activated during the 2024 protest crisis. Signal: confidential-adviser network.
rationale.EMP: Provided mental-health support after the encampment clearance, but many students/staff felt the violent police response showed limited empathy. Signal: post-protest support vs. police clearance.
rationale.ACT: Mobilized psychological support quickly during the May 2024 events; commissioned an independent investigation. Signal: independent investigation commissioned.
rationale.EQU: Dutch access model, salaried PhDs, and strong diversity/inclusion infrastructure give solid equity baseline. Signal: salaried PhD + Dutch access.
rationale.BND: PhD-as-employee status protects grad workers; but the choice to call riot police on a campus encampment raised consent/autonomy concerns. Signal: salaried PhDs vs. police escalation.
rationale.ACC: Commissioned an independent review of the May 6-17 events and published follow-up steps — partial accountability, though calling police was widely criticized. Signal: independent review + published steps.
rationale.SYS: Strong critical-social-science tradition and public engagement on structural issues. Signal: structural research mission.
rationale.INT: Gap between stated dialogue/safety values and the decision to deploy riot police created a visible integrity tension. Signal: dialogue-values vs. police response.

name: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill

rationale.AWR: After 2021 student suicides exposed gaps, UNC built the Heels Care Network and expanded CAPS; CAPS historically tracked only its own users, a documented blind spot. Signal: post-crisis Heels Care Network.
rationale.EMP: Crisis response (class cancellation, mental-health summit) showed acknowledgment, but students said counseling needed real improvement. Signal: 2021 mental-health summit.
rationale.ACT: CAPS expanded staffing and added short-term capacity amid demand surge and national shortage; wait times remained a concern. Signal: CAPS staffing expansion.
rationale.EQU: Carolina Covenant (debt-free for low-income students) is a strong, long-running access program; but UNC was the named party in SFFA v. UNC, ending race-conscious admissions. Signal: Carolina Covenant.
rationale.BND: No grad-worker contract (NC bars public-sector collective bargaining); student-worker protections weak. Signal: no collective bargaining permitted.
rationale.ACC: UNC does not track campuswide suicides and faced criticism for the gap; response to the crisis was reactive. The Nikole Hannah-Jones tenure episode also damaged accountability perceptions. Signal: untracked suicides + tenure controversy.
rationale.SYS: Carolina Covenant shows root-cause affordability work; broader structural advocacy mixed under political pressure on the UNC System. Signal: affordability program.
rationale.INT: Board-of-Governors political interference (tenure, DEI rollbacks) created visible gaps between stated academic-freedom values and governance behavior. Signal: governance interference.

rationale.AWR: Danish APV workplace psychosocial assessments are legally required, but UCPH cancelled/postponed satisfaction and wellbeing surveys to save money amid cuts — weakening detection. Signal: cancelled wellbeing surveys.
rationale.EMP: Student counseling and study-environment support exist; union reps report cuts hit working conditions and morale. Signal: union-reported morale impact.
rationale.ACT: Counseling and student-services provision is solid in the Danish welfare context. Signal: welfare-state student services.
rationale.EQU: Tuition-free for EU students, state grants (SU) for living costs, and salaried PhD positions give very strong baseline equity. Signal: tuition-free + SU + salaried PhDs.
rationale.BND: Salaried PhD/employee model and strong Danish labor protections give robust boundaries, though the 2024 cuts (~380 FTE) created insecurity. Signal: strong labor protection vs. layoffs.
rationale.ACC: Administration-reform cuts handled through formal consultation; cancelling wellbeing surveys during cuts is an accountability concern. Signal: consultation process vs. survey cancellation.
rationale.SYS: Strong long-horizon public-research planning and warnings about long-term capacity loss from PhD cuts. Signal: long-horizon capacity analysis.
rationale.INT: Cutting the very surveys that measure staff wellbeing while imposing 500+ job cuts creates a values-behavior gap; scored cautiously, medium confidence. Signal: cost-cuts vs. wellbeing-measurement.

rationale.AWR: CAPS runs an innovative open-access embedded model plus TimelyCare virtual care for all students; layered detection. Signal: CAPS embedded model + TimelyCare.
rationale.EMP: Coordinated 'healing and recovery' presidential response and affinity-center/chaplaincy support indicate affective care; some Title IX-mishandling lawsuits remain. Signal: presidential healing effort.
rationale.ACT: Mental-health access expanded with free virtual care; responsive crisis stabilization. Signal: free TimelyCare access.
rationale.EQU: Need-blind for domestic and (from Class of 2029) international students, 100% demonstrated need met, and a landmark 2003 slavery-and-justice acknowledgment plus reparative steps. Signal: need-blind expansion + Slavery & Justice report legacy.
rationale.BND: GLO was the first Ivy grad union to win a contract (stipend increases, protections) — strong enforceable labor boundaries. Signal: first Ivy grad-worker contract.
rationale.ACC: Brown's 2006 Slavery and Justice report and ongoing commitments are an early, substantive harm-acknowledgment model; recent Title IX lawsuits temper the score. Signal: Slavery & Justice report + ongoing repair.
rationale.SYS: Reparative-justice and access reforms show root-cause and structural orientation. Signal: structural reparative work.
rationale.INT: Generally consistent between stated values and behavior (need-blind, union recognition, historical-harm work); litigation exposure keeps it from a higher score. Signal: values-behavior consistency.

rationale.AWR: Counseling and wellbeing services and a Master's in Mental Health exist; post-2019 the campus was a site of acute student trauma. Signal: wellbeing services + MH academic program.
rationale.EMP: The 2019 siege left lasting trauma; institutional empathy toward affected students was constrained by the political/security environment. Signal: post-siege trauma context.
rationale.ACT: Counseling provision is reasonable; trauma-specific follow-through after 2019 is not well documented. Signal: counseling provision.
rationale.EQU: Need-based aid and English-medium access; local widening-access data limited in English. Signal: financial-aid provision.
rationale.BND: Postgraduate stipends competitive; broader autonomy constrained by the NSL environment. Signal: RPg stipend support.
rationale.ACC: Operating under the NSL and Safeguarding National Security Ordinance, independent harm acknowledgment on contested events is severely constrained (HRW 2024). Signal: NSL constraint on accountability.
rationale.SYS: Structural critique/advocacy is heavily constrained by the academic-freedom environment in Hong Kong. Signal: academic-freedom decline.
rationale.INT: Welfare practices may be genuine, but the external security regime limits values-expression; scored conservatively, medium confidence — the constraint is contextual, not penalizing institutional intent.

rationale.AWR: Counseling Center offers free individual/group counseling, psychological testing and suicide-prevention education, with English/Chinese-language services for international students. Signal: free counseling + suicide-prevention programming.
rationale.EMP: Gender-equity and counseling structures exist; affective-care evidence is limited in English. Signal: counseling + gender-equity offices.
rationale.ACT: Free counseling and a Global One-Stop Service Center for international students indicate reasonable responsiveness. Signal: One-Stop Service Center.
rationale.EQU: Korean private-university tuition is high and need-based aid less extensive than US elites; access-gap data limited. Signal: high private tuition.
rationale.BND: Part-time/adjunct lecturers lack a fixed wage scale or seniority raises, with wages near minimum — documented precarity. Signal: adjunct wage precarity.
rationale.ACC: Standard Korean Title-equivalent gender-equity processes; public harm-disclosure record thin in English. Signal: gender-equity process.
rationale.SYS: Limited public evidence of structural advocacy; conservative score. Signal: thin structural-advocacy evidence.
rationale.INT: As a private institution with notable adjunct precarity alongside strong student services, there is a worker-vs-student care gap; medium confidence given partial English evidence. Signal: adjunct-vs-student care gap.

rationale.AWR: Operates an MOE-mandated psychological counseling center; disaggregated detection data not public. Conservative under low English evidence. Signal: mandated counseling center.
rationale.EMP: No public independent advocacy disclosure; supervisor-power asymmetry is a documented sector-wide risk. Signal: hierarchical supervisor model.
rationale.ACT: Counseling/emergency-aid per national policy; utilization data not public. Low confidence.
rationale.EQU: National financial-aid and rural-quota access schemes apply; institution-specific equity reporting limited. Signal: national access schemes.
rationale.BND: Documented abusive-supervision and overwork risks across Chinese grad programs; no enforceable union mechanism. Signal: abusive-supervision risk.
rationale.ACC: Limited public harm-acknowledgment/repair mechanisms; weak whistleblower protection in sector. Signal: weak public accountability.
rationale.SYS: State long-horizon planning strong; independent structural critique constrained. Signal: state planning without independent critique.
rationale.INT: Values alignment not independently verifiable; scored conservatively with low confidence due to limited public/English disclosure.

rationale.AWR: Boynton/Student Counseling Services and a published EOT annual report give structured detection; COGS maintains student-led wellbeing resources. Signal: counseling services + EOT annual report.
rationale.EMP: Equal Opportunity & Title IX office operates professionally with a public annual report; affective resonance mixed across a very large system. Signal: EOT public reporting.
rationale.ACT: Counseling and emergency-aid (student emergency funds) provision; capacity strained at scale. Signal: emergency-aid funds.
rationale.EQU: Strong land-grant access mission, U Promise aid, and a notable land-grant/Indigenous-land reckoning (TRUTH Project with Dakota/Ojibwe nations). Signal: U Promise + TRUTH Project.
rationale.BND: GLU-UE graduate workers won their first contract in 2024-25 after a credible strike threat — new enforceable labor boundaries; Teamsters staff also organized. Signal: first GLU-UE contract.
rationale.ACC: EOT publishes an annual report (some transparency); but a federal Title IX referral to DOJ and Title VI investigations indicate compliance disputes. Signal: DOE/HHS Title IX referral.
rationale.SYS: Land-grant public-good mission and the TRUTH Project show root-cause/historical-harm orientation. Signal: TRUTH Project structural reckoning.
rationale.INT: Generally aligned land-grant values, but a recent federal Title IX non-compliance referral creates a values-behavior tension. Signal: federal non-compliance referral.

rationale.AWR: Counseling and wellbeing services exist and have expanded, but the Tyndall case showed catastrophic failure to detect/act on years of complaints. Signal: Tyndall detection failure.
rationale.EMP: OCR found USC failed to respond promptly to at least nine Tyndall complaints; a separate Dennis Kelly case showed LGBT+ students' reports ignored — severe empathy/validation failures. Signal: ignored survivor complaints.
rationale.ACT: Counseling expansion and a $1.1B Tyndall settlement represent eventual action, but only after decades of inaction. Signal: $1.1B settlement (delayed action).
rationale.EQU: Strong financial-aid expansion (free tuition for families under income thresholds) and large first-gen enrollment. Signal: tuition-free aid threshold.
rationale.BND: Consent and patient-safety boundaries were egregiously breached in the Tyndall/Kelly cases; grad-worker organizing ongoing. Signal: consent-boundary breaches.
rationale.ACC: OCR found USC violated Title IX; lawsuits alleged the conduct office destroyed/hid records and retaliated against whistleblowers — a serious, adjudicated accountability failure despite the record settlement. Signal: OCR Title IX violation + records-concealment allegations.
rationale.SYS: Aid reforms show some root-cause work, but governance scandals (admissions bribery, dean drug scandal) indicate systemic culture problems. Signal: multiple governance scandals.
rationale.INT: Repeated, documented gaps between stated values and conduct (Tyndall cover-up, admissions scandal) place integrity in the low range despite eventual large settlements. Signal: pattern of cover-up vs. stated values.

rationale.AWR: CAPS and basic-needs/food-security programs provide detection; the acute housing crisis is a documented, partly-unaddressed source of student distress. Signal: housing-crisis distress.
rationale.EMP: Basic-needs and CARE advocacy services exist; the housing crisis and 2020 COLA strike showed students felt unheard on cost-of-living. Signal: 2020 COLA wildcat strike.
rationale.ACT: CAPS and emergency aid operate; UCSB abandoned the criticized windowless Munger Hall design after expert/student pushback and is building new beds. Signal: Munger Hall cancellation + new housing.
rationale.EQU: Hispanic-Serving Institution and AANAPISI with very high Pell-student share and strong UC access/financial-aid (Blue and Gold) — among the most equitable in the tranche. Signal: HSI status + high Pell share.
rationale.BND: UC-wide UAW 2022 strike (48,000 academic workers, largest US higher-ed strike) won major stipend/benefit gains applying to UCSB grad workers. Signal: UAW 2022 contract gains.
rationale.ACC: Responded to expert criticism by killing Munger Hall; UC system Title IX/Clery compliance is structured. Signal: design reversal after expert report.
rationale.SYS: HSI mission, basic-needs infrastructure, and UC public-good advocacy show structural orientation; housing root-cause only partly addressed. Signal: basic-needs + access mission.
rationale.INT: UC values broadly reflected in access and labor outcomes, but chronic housing failure and contested Munger episode keep integrity mid-range. Signal: access values vs. housing failure.

rationale.AWR: University Counseling Center plus a coordinated Student Care Network and Project Safe give layered, well-funded detection. Signal: Student Care Network coordination.
rationale.EMP: UCC and Project Safe coordinate empathetic navigation across services; affective resonance reported as solid for a private of its scale. Signal: Project Safe navigation.
rationale.ACT: Counseling implemented a goal-directed model to extend reach; psychological consultation for grad students/postdocs. Signal: expanded goal-directed care model.
rationale.EQU: Opportunity Vanderbilt (no-loan, meets full need) and strong first-gen/Pell support make access a genuine strength. Signal: Opportunity Vanderbilt no-loan aid.
rationale.BND: No graduate-worker union/contract; private-institution labor protections weaker than unionized peers. Signal: no grad-worker contract.
rationale.ACC: Functional Title IX/Student Discrimination Office and Clery compliance; no recent landmark adverse OCR resolution found. Signal: compliant Title IX posture.
rationale.SYS: Affordability reform shows root-cause work; broader structural advocacy is muted (institution emphasizes 'institutional neutrality'). Signal: neutrality stance limits advocacy.
rationale.INT: Strong consistency on access values; the neutrality posture and absence of worker bargaining create a moderate values-scope limit. Signal: access consistency vs. limited advocacy.
